text.title: Legal Fundamentals for Founders: ToS, Privacy, and Trademark Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-legal-basics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Ship Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and at least one IP-protection artefact (trademark filing or copyright notice) BEFORE accepting the first paying customer. Operating without these is forbidden.

rationale.source: Online-business legal practice (Bloomberg Law, Cooley GO templates)

rule.rationale: Operating without a ToS / Privacy Policy exposes the operator to unlimited liability and regulator action (GDPR, CCPA fines start at the first user). The floor is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: ToS MUST include: payment terms, billing cycles, IP ownership, disclaimers, liability cap, termination, and governing-law clause. Missing any of these renders the ToS unenforceable in most disputes.
rule.rationale: Courts routinely strike incomplete ToS as unconscionable or unenforceable. The seven clauses above are the operational floor for any consumer-facing agreement.

rule.statement: Privacy Policy MUST disclose: data categories collected, purposes, retention period, user rights (access / deletion / portability), and security measures. Vague language ('we may collect') is forbidden.
rule.rationale: GDPR and CCPA require specific, layered disclosure with user-rights language. Vague disclosure is treated as no disclosure under enforcement actions.

rule.statement: File for trademark registration on the brand name BEFORE any paid marketing campaign that uses the mark prominently. Spending on a mark before filing exposes the operator to squatting and infringement risk.
rule.rationale: Common-law trademark rights are narrow; registered marks get nationwide protection. Pre-spend filing is the cheapest insurance against losing the brand to a faster registrant.

rule.statement: Confirm jurisdiction-specific requirements (GDPR / CCPA / COPPA / ADA) per the markets the product accepts customers from. US-only ToS for an EU-accessible site is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Regulators apply law based on customer location, not operator location. EU-accessible products must comply with GDPR regardless of where the operator is.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
